description: NIP-59 seal + gift wrap test vectors for NOSTR Mail. Because NIP-44 encryption uses random nonces and gift wraps use ephemeral keys, exact ciphertext cannot be tested. Instead, verify structure, round-trip correctness, and signature validity.

vectors.name: seal creation
vectors.description: Create a kind 13 seal event by NIP-44 encrypting a rumor to the recipient. The seal is signed by the sender.

expected_structure.content: <NIP-44 encrypted JSON string of the rumor>

checks: seal.kind == 13
seal.pubkey == sender's pubkey (Alice)
seal.tags == [] (empty array — no metadata leakage)
seal.content is a valid NIP-44 ciphertext string
seal.id is a valid 32-byte hex SHA-256 hash
seal.sig is a valid Schnorr signature for seal.pubkey over seal.id
NIP-44 decrypt(seal.content, sender_privkey, recipient_pubkey) == JSON.stringify(rumor)
seal.created_at may be randomized (not necessarily matching rumor.created_at)

vectors.name: gift wrap creation
vectors.description: Create a kind 1059 gift wrap by generating an ephemeral key, NIP-44 encrypting the seal to the recipient using the ephemeral key, and signing with the ephemeral key.

input.note: An ephemeral keypair is generated fresh for each gift wrap. The ephemeral privkey is discarded after signing.

expected_structure.content: <NIP-44 encrypted JSON string of the seal>
expected_structure.sig: <Schnorr signature valid for the ephemeral pubkey>

checks: wrap.kind == 1059
wrap.pubkey is a valid 32-byte hex public key (ephemeral, not Alice's or Bob's)
wrap.tags has exactly one 'p' tag matching the recipient's pubkey
wrap.content is a valid NIP-44 ciphertext string
wrap.sig is a valid Schnorr signature for wrap.pubkey (the ephemeral key) over wrap.id
wrap.created_at is randomized (within a reasonable range, e.g., +/- 48 hours)
NIP-44 decrypt(wrap.content, recipient_privkey, ephemeral_pubkey) == JSON.stringify(seal)

vectors.name: full round-trip: create, wrap, unwrap, verify
vectors.description: Alice creates a mail rumor, seals it, wraps it for Bob. Bob unwraps, unseals, and verifies the original rumor matches.

original_rumor.content: This message should survive seal+wrap+unwrap+unseal intact.

steps.description: NIP-44 encrypt(JSON.stringify(rumor), sender_privkey, recipient_pubkey) -> seal.content. Sign seal with sender_privkey.
Generate ephemeral keypair. NIP-44 encrypt(JSON.stringify(seal), ephemeral_privkey, recipient_pubkey) -> wrap.content. Sign wrap with ephemeral_privkey. Add ['p', recipient_pubkey] tag.
Bob receives the kind 1059 event. The 'p' tag matches his pubkey.
NIP-44 decrypt(wrap.content, recipient_privkey, wrap.pubkey) -> seal JSON. Parse seal. Verify seal.sig is valid for seal.pubkey (reveals sender is Alice).
NIP-44 decrypt(seal.content, recipient_privkey, seal.pubkey) -> rumor JSON. Parse rumor.
Assert recovered rumor fields match the original exactly.

expected_after_round_trip.recovered_rumor.content: This message should survive seal+wrap+unwrap+unseal intact.

vectors.name: multi-recipient wrapping
vectors.description: Alice sends the same mail to Bob and Charlie. The same rumor is sealed once per recipient and wrapped once per recipient. Each recipient gets a distinct gift wrap with a distinct ephemeral key.

rumor.content: Hi Bob and Charlie, this goes to both of you.

checks: Two separate gift wrap events are produced (one per recipient)
wrap_for_bob.tags == [['p', bob_pubkey]]
wrap_for_charlie.tags == [['p', charlie_pubkey]]
wrap_for_bob.pubkey != wrap_for_charlie.pubkey (different ephemeral keys)
wrap_for_bob.id != wrap_for_charlie.id (different events entirely)
wrap_for_bob.content != wrap_for_charlie.content (different ciphertexts)
Bob decrypts his wrap -> seal -> rumor == original rumor
Charlie decrypts his wrap -> seal -> rumor == original rumor
Bob CANNOT decrypt Charlie's gift wrap (wrong recipient key)
Charlie CANNOT decrypt Bob's gift wrap (wrong recipient key)
Both recovered rumors are field-for-field identical

vectors.name: self-copy wrapping
vectors.description: Alice sends a message to Bob and also wraps a copy for herself (sender's outbox copy). This allows Alice to see her sent messages on other devices.

rumor.content: Bob gets this, and Alice keeps a copy too.

wrap_for_bob.note: Standard gift wrap to Bob. Seal encrypted to Bob's pubkey.

wrap_for_alice_self.note: Self-copy: seal is encrypted to Alice's own pubkey. Gift wrap 'p' tag is Alice's pubkey. Published to Alice's inbox relays.

checks: Two gift wraps produced: one for Bob, one for Alice (self)
Self-copy wrap.tags[0] == ['p', alice_pubkey]
Self-copy uses a different ephemeral key than Bob's wrap
Self-copy seal is encrypted with Alice as both sender and recipient
Alice can decrypt her self-copy: unwrap -> unseal -> original rumor
The recovered rumor from Alice's self-copy matches the original exactly
Bob CANNOT decrypt Alice's self-copy wrap
Relays see two gift wraps with different 'p' tags — they cannot tell one is a self-copy
